title: Anti-Ragging Policy
what_constitutes_ragging: Any conduct by students involving teasing, rude behavior, or improper handling of a fresher or another student.
Engaging in disorderly or undisciplined activities causing annoyance, hardship, physical or psychological harm, or fear.
Forcing a student to perform acts causing shame, embarrassment, or mental distress.
Any act by a senior that disrupts academic activities of other students.
Exploiting students to complete academic tasks assigned to others.
Financial extortion or forcing students to spend money.
Physical abuse including sexual abuse, assault, stripping, or any act causing bodily harm.
Verbal or written abuse including emails, posts, or public insults causing humiliation or distress.
Any act affecting mental health, self-confidence, or dignity, including abuse of power or authority.
punishments: Withdrawal or withholding of scholarship/fellowship and other benefits
Debarment from examinations or evaluation processes
Debarment from representing the institution in events or competitions
Expulsion from the institution and ban from admission elsewhere
FIR to be filed by the institution
